message:
feat(obligation/saga): add sheaf-theoretic consistency checking for saga batches

Add `check_sheaf_consistency()` to SagaExecutionResult to detect global
inconsistencies that pairwise lattice merges hide.

Problem: when a saga runs coordination-free batches, each batch produces a
local view of obligation state via its `merged_state`. Pairwise lattice
merges can conceal "phantom commits" — situations where the merged state
appears terminal but no single batch witnessed the full commit sequence.

Solution: model each batch as a sheaf node with a snapshot of the
obligations it touched, then run the SagaConsistencyChecker to detect
cohomological obstructions (H^1 != 0). This catches inconsistencies that
are invisible to pairwise merge validation.

Implementation details:
- Each batch becomes a NodeSnapshot keyed by "batch-{i}" with observed
  obligation states from its merged_state.
- When `obligation_ids` are provided, each batch observes its actual
  obligation set; without them, each batch gets a synthetic single
  obligation (still detects state disagreements but not phantom commits).
- Default constraint: AllOrNothing atomicity — all obligations in the saga
  must reach the same terminal state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
